articles.title: Everybody wants to rule the AI world
articles.summary: Sam Altman's 2024 ouster from OpenAI was chaotic, with the CEO selection process involving video calls and texts between current and former leadership, as revealed in the ongoing Musk v. Altman trial.

articles.title: Mira Murati’s deposition pulled back the curtain on Sam Altman’s ouster
articles.summary: Sam Altman's 2023 ouster from OpenAI for being 'not consistently candid' is now detailed through Mira Murati's deposition in the Musk v. Altman trial, revealing behind-the-scenes details of the dramatic power struggle.

articles.title: Apple’s AirPods with cameras for AI are apparently close to production
articles.summary: Apple's camera-equipped AirPods are advancing toward mass production, currently in design validation testing. The cameras are designed for AI features rather than photography.

articles.title: Why you can never get your doctor to call you back
articles.summary: AI company Basata automates administrative tasks to help overwhelmed medical staff handle workload. While automation may eventually displace workers, current staff appreciate relief from drowning in administrative duties.

articles.title: OpenAI launches new voice intelligence features in its API
articles.summary: OpenAI launches voice intelligence features in its API for customer service, education, and creator platforms. The new capabilities expand AI applications across multiple industries.

articles.title: Voi founders’ new AI startup Pit has become the latest rising star out of Stockholm
articles.summary: Pit, a new AI startup founded by Voi's co-founders, has raised $16 million in seed funding led by a16z. The Stockholm-based company is emerging as a notable AI venture from Europe.

articles.title: Railway secures $100 million to challenge AWS with AI-native cloud infrastructure
articles.summary: Railway, a San Francisco cloud platform with 2 million developers, raised $100 million Series B to build AI-native infrastructure competing against AWS and Google Cloud by addressing legacy platform limitations.

articles.title: Claude Code costs up to $200 a month. Goose does the same thing for free.
articles.summary: Claude Code's $20-$200 monthly pricing has frustrated developers, but Goose, an open-source alternative by Block, offers identical coding features for free and runs locally without subscription fees.

articles.title: Listen Labs raises $69M after viral billboard hiring stunt to scale AI customer interviews
articles.summary: Listen Labs raised $69M Series B after a viral billboard hiring campaign featuring AI tokens. The startup decoded the puzzle to recruit engineers through an algorithmic challenge.

articles.title: Mozilla says 271 vulnerabilities found by Mythos have "almost no false positives"
articles.summary: Mozilla revealed that its AI vulnerability detector, Anthropic's Mythos, found 271 Firefox security flaws with minimal false positives. The breakthrough demonstrates AI's effectiveness in identifying zero-day vulnerabilities.

articles.title: Ars Asks: Share your shell and show us your tricked-out terminals!
articles.summary: Terminal usage has increased significantly despite predictions of graphical interfaces replacing command-line tools. The command line remains superior for many tasks compared to mouse-driven point-and-click interfaces.

articles.title: Widely used Daemon Tools disk app backdoored in monthlong supply-chain attack
articles.summary: Daemon Tools disk mounting app was backdoored for a month through a supply-chain attack, with malicious updates signed by the developer's certificate affecting Windows versions 12.5.0.2421-12.5.0.2434.

articles.title: The Download: AI malaise and babymaking tech
articles.summary: AI is becoming ubiquitous but society faces uncertainty about its impacts on jobs, the economy, and daily life. People feel uncomfortable with widespread AI integration despite unclear benefits or risks.

articles.title: Here’s how technology transformed babymaking
articles.summary: Technology has revolutionized fertility treatment since the first IVF baby in 1978. Emerging innovations include AI, robotics, and gene-edited embryos alongside improved hormonal treatments and genetic testing.

articles.title: The Download: the tech reshaping IVF and the rise of balcony solar
articles.summary: New technologies including AI, robotics, and genetic editing are transforming IVF to make it faster, more effective, and accessible. However, these innovations raise significant ethical concerns alongside their potential benefits.

articles.title: MedQA: Fine-Tuning a Clinical AI on AMD ROCm — No CUDA Required
articles.summary: MedQA demonstrates fine-tuning clinical AI models using AMD ROCm instead of NVIDIA CUDA. This approach provides an alternative compute platform for medical AI development without proprietary GPU requirements.

articles.title: vLLM V0 to V1: Correctness Before Corrections in RL
articles.summary: vLLM updates from V0 to V1 prioritize correctness in reinforcement learning before making corrections. The focus ensures accurate model behavior and reliable performance improvements.

articles.title: Adding Benchmaxxer Repellant to the Open ASR Leaderboard
articles.summary: The Open ASR Leaderboard now includes Benchmaxxer Repellant to prevent gaming results through overfitting. This addition maintains fair evaluation standards for speech recognition systems.

articles.title: A conversation with Kevin Scott: What’s next in AI
articles.summary: Kevin Scott discusses the future direction of artificial intelligence development and emerging trends in the AI field.

articles.title: From Hot Wheels to handling content: How brands are using Microsoft AI to be more productive and imaginative
articles.summary: Brands like Hot Wheels are leveraging Microsoft AI tools to enhance productivity and creativity in content creation and marketing strategies.

articles.title: Microsoft open sources its ‘farm of the future’ toolkit
articles.summary: Microsoft has open-sourced its 'farm of the future' toolkit, making agricultural technology tools publicly available to advance smart farming practices and sustainability.

articles.title: 5 gardening tips you can try right in Search
articles.summary: Google's AI Mode, Search Live, and Shopping features offer five gardening tips to help plants thrive. Users can leverage these tools for plant care guidance and gardening resources.

articles.title: Google is partnering with XPRIZE and Range Media Partners on the $3.5 million Future Vision film competition.
articles.summary: Google partners with XPRIZE and Range Media Partners to launch the Future Vision film competition, offering $3.5 million in prizes for filmmakers to create content exploring future possibilities.

articles.title: The latest AI news we announced in April 2026
articles.summary: Google announced its latest AI updates in April 2026, though specific details are not provided in the article content shown.

articles.title: AlphaEvolve: How our Gemini-powered coding agent is scaling impact across fields
articles.summary: AlphaEvolve uses Gemini-powered algorithms to create a coding agent that scales impact across business, infrastructure, and scientific fields.

articles.title: Enabling a new model for healthcare with AI co-clinician
articles.summary: Researchers are developing an AI co-clinician to augment healthcare delivery. The initiative explores how AI can assist medical professionals in patient care.

articles.title: Announcing our partnership with the Republic of Korea
articles.summary: Google DeepMind partners with the Republic of Korea to accelerate scientific breakthroughs using advanced AI models.

articles.title: Last Week in AI #340 - OpenAI vs Musk + Microsoft, DeepSeek v4, Vision Banana
articles.summary: Elon Musk testified in his legal case against OpenAI, claiming the nonprofit was stolen. The trial revealed evidence of model training practices and settlement discussions between parties.

articles.title: LWiAI Podcast #243 - GPT 5.5, DeepSeek V4, AI safety sabotage
articles.summary: Podcast discusses OpenAI's GPT-5.5 release with coding improvements and higher pricing, xAI's Grok Voice Think Fast 1.0 for real-time voice agents, and recent AI safety concerns.

articles.title: LWiAI Podcast #242 - ChatGPT Images 2.0, Qwen 3.6 Max, Kimi-K2.6
articles.summary: The podcast discusses OpenAI's improved ChatGPT image model with better text accuracy, alongside updates on Qwen 3.6 Max and Kimi-K2.6, reflecting rapid AI development across image generation and language models.

articles.title: AI helping ease the UK’s NHS burden
articles.summary: AI-enabled virtual care is being deployed in the UK NHS to alleviate strain on hospitals and reduce waiting lists by managing patients in community settings.

articles.title: HP and the art of AI and data for the enterprise
articles.summary: HP discusses enterprise AI and data challenges, exploring how companies can leverage first-party data effectively. The article covers cloud versus local compute decisions and the importance of proper data preparation for AI models.

articles.title: US government increases AI suppliers and rethinks Anthropic’s role
articles.summary: The US Pentagon expanded its AI supplier roster to include Microsoft, Amazon, Nvidia, and Reflection AI for classified operations, while canceling a $200 million Anthropic contract over disagreements about surveillance and weapons use restrictions.

articles.title: Which Agent Causes Task Failures and When?Researchers from PSU and Duke explores automated failure attribution of LLM Multi-Agent Systems
articles.summary: Researchers from PSU and Duke developed methods to automatically identify which agent causes task failures in LLM multi-agent systems and when failures occur, addressing a key challenge in collaborative AI systems.

articles.title: ByteDance Introduces Astra: A Dual-Model Architecture for Autonomous Robot Navigation
articles.summary: ByteDance introduces Astra, a dual-model architecture for robot navigation that addresses localization, self-positioning, and path planning challenges in complex indoor environments.

articles.title: MIT Researchers Unveil “SEAL”: A New Step Towards Self-Improving AI
articles.summary: MIT researchers introduced SEAL, a framework enabling large language models to self-improve by generating training data through self-editing and updating their own weights via reinforcement learning.

articles.title: How Memory Sparse Attention scales LLM memory to 100 million tokens
articles.summary: Memory Sparse Attention (MSA) enables large language models to process up to 100 million tokens while maintaining accuracy, significantly expanding context window capabilities.

articles.title: Claude Code is leaking API keys into public package registries
articles.summary: Claude Code and similar AI coding assistants are inadvertently leaking sensitive API keys to public package registries, posing significant security risks.

articles.title: Anthropic’s MCP vulnerability: When ‘expected behavior’ becomes a supply chain nightmare
articles.summary: Anthropic's Model Context Protocol has a critical architectural flaw exposing millions of AI applications to remote takeovers, presenting a major supply chain security risk.

articles.title: Anthropic Introduces Natural Language Autoencoders That Convert Claude’s Internal Activations Directly into Human-Readable Text Explanations
articles.summary: Anthropic introduced Natural Language Autoencoders (NLAs) that convert Claude's internal activations into human-readable text explanations, making AI model thinking more interpretable to non-experts.

articles.title: OpenAI Releases Three Realtime Audio Models: GPT-Realtime-2, GPT-Realtime-Translate, and GPT-Realtime-Whisper in the Realtime API
articles.summary: OpenAI released three realtime audio models: GPT-Realtime-2 for voice reasoning, GPT-Realtime-Translate for live translation, and GPT-Realtime-Whisper for transcription. The Realtime API is now generally available for production use.

articles.title: Build a CloakBrowser Automation Workflow with Stealth Chromium, Persistent Profiles, and Browser Signal Inspection
articles.summary: CloakBrowser is a Python browser automation tool using stealth Chromium with Playwright-style APIs. The tutorial covers setup, context creation, session persistence, profile management, and content extraction.

articles.title: Dell vs. Lenovo: I've tested dozens of laptops from both brands, and here's my pick
articles.summary: Dell and Lenovo are compared across their laptop offerings to help consumers choose the best brand for their needs based on extensive testing.

articles.title: After using Lenovo's $2,600 Yoga, I'm taking premium Windows laptops seriously again
articles.summary: Lenovo's $2,600 Yoga Pro 9i Aura Edition impresses with strong performance, vivid display, and robust cooling, renewing interest in premium Windows laptops.

articles.title: Roku apps loading slow? 9 quick fixes I try before blaming my Wi-Fi
articles.summary: Roku app slowness may not be Wi-Fi related. The article provides quick troubleshooting fixes to improve device performance before blaming internet connection.

articles.title: Implementing Permission-Gated Tool Calling in Python Agents
articles.summary: AI agents now implement permission-gated tool calling in Python, allowing controlled access to functions based on user permissions rather than operating as passive chatbots.

articles.title: The Roadmap to Mastering Tool Calling in AI Agents
articles.summary: The article provides guidance on implementing tool calling in AI agents, enabling them to interact with external systems and APIs effectively to enhance their capabilities and functionality.

articles.title: Implementing Statistical Guardrails for Non-Deterministic Agents
articles.summary: Statistical guardrails help manage non-deterministic agents where identical inputs produce varying outputs. These safeguards ensure consistent, reliable behavior across multiple runs.

articles.title: When Customers Churn at Renewal: Was It the Price or the Project?
articles.summary: A practitioner's guide for determining causal attribution when customers churn at renewal due to multiple factors like pricing or project issues.

articles.title: Unified Agentic Memory Across Harnesses Using Hooks
articles.summary: Hook implementation enables Claude Code, Codex, and Cursor to share persistent memory via Neo4j without vendor lock-in, allowing unified agentic memory across different AI development harnesses.

articles.title: The Joy of Typing
articles.summary: A practical guide exploring modern type annotations in Python for data science applications, helping developers write clearer and more maintainable code.

articles.title: Powering the Next American Century: US Energy Secretary Chris Wright and NVIDIA’s Ian Buck on the Genesis Mission
articles.summary: US Energy Secretary Chris Wright and NVIDIA's Ian Buck discussed how AI will drive energy innovation needed for American technological leadership. They emphasized that affordable energy access is critical for societal opportunities and economic competitiveness.

articles.title: Linked and Loaded: Gaijin Single Sign-On Now Available on GeForce NOW
articles.summary: GeForce NOW now supports Gaijin Single Sign-On for faster logins to Gaijin games. Seven new games were added to the cloud gaming platform this week.

articles.title: NVIDIA Spectrum-X — the Open, AI-Native Ethernet Fabric — Sets the Standard for Gigascale AI, Now With MRC
articles.summary: NVIDIA Spectrum-X Ethernet fabric is the leading AI networking technology for building powerful AI factories, deployed by major companies like OpenAI, Microsoft, and Oracle.

articles.title: Halliburton enhances seismic workflow creation with Amazon Bedrock and Generative AI
articles.summary: Halliburton partnered with AWS to develop an AI-powered assistant using Amazon Bedrock that simplifies seismic workflow creation, reducing the manual configuration of complex processing tools and improving accessibility.

articles.title: Secure short-term GPU capacity for ML workloads with EC2 Capacity Blocks for ML and SageMaker training plans
articles.summary: AWS introduces EC2 Capacity Blocks for ML and SageMaker training plans to address GPU scarcity, enabling companies to secure reliable short-term GPU capacity for machine learning workloads.

articles.title: Overcoming reward signal challenges: Verifiable rewards-based reinforcement learning with GRPO on SageMaker AI
articles.summary: This article explores reinforcement learning with verifiable rewards (RLVR) to improve LLM training by introducing transparency into reward signals, addressing reliability challenges in traditional RL approaches.

articles.title: Achieving Peak System and Workload Efficiency on NVIDIA GB200 NVL72 with Slurm Block Scheduling
articles.summary: NVIDIA GB200 NVL72 extends NVLink coherence across entire racks for exascale performance, requiring new scheduling approaches like Slurm block scheduling to optimize workload efficiency.

articles.title: Model Quantization: Post-Training Quantization Using NVIDIA Model Optimizer
articles.summary: Model quantization reduces VRAM usage and improves inference performance on NVIDIA GPUs by lowering computational and memory requirements while preserving model quality for resource-constrained environments.

articles.title: Real-Time Performance Monitoring and Faster Debugging with NCCL Inspector and Prometheus
articles.summary: NVIDIA introduces NCCL Inspector and Prometheus integration for real-time performance monitoring and faster debugging of GPU-to-GPU communication in distributed deep learning.

articles.title: How generational differences affect consumer attitudes towards ads
articles.summary: Research study examines how different generations perceive social media advertisements across various platforms, revealing distinct attitudes toward ads based on age demographics.

articles.title: Every tree counts
articles.summary: Meta aims for net zero emissions by 2030, developing and openly sharing technology to reduce its carbon footprint.

articles.title: How a non-traditional background led to cutting-edge XR tech
articles.summary: An individual's unconventional background enabled them to develop innovative extended reality technology, demonstrating how diverse experiences can drive technological breakthroughs in the XR industry.

articles.title: Gradient-based Planning for World Models at Longer Horizons
articles.summary: GRASP is a gradient-based planner for world models enabling long-horizon planning through parallel trajectory optimization, stochastic exploration, and improved gradient shaping for vision-based control.

articles.title: Identifying Interactions at Scale for LLMs
articles.summary: Researchers are developing methods to understand LLM behavior at scale through feature attribution and interpretability techniques, advancing transparency and safety in complex AI systems.

articles.title: Information-Driven Design of Imaging Systems
articles.summary: Research presents an information-driven approach to designing imaging systems that optimizes how well measurements distinguish between objects using noise models and encoders.
